syzygium:congolense.caption: PL. eugenia:obanensis.caption: PL. IV. — Eugenia fernandopona Engler (Klaine 3045) : 1, tige et boutons (× 1/2); 2, fleur (? ♂) (× 3); 3, fleur (? ♀) (× 4); 4, fruit (× 2); 5, base de la feuille (× 2). — Eugenia obanensis Bak. f. (Fleury 26339) : 6, fruit (× 4); 7, base de la feuille.

octolepis:decalepis.caption: PL. VI. — Octolepis decalepis Gilg : 1, rameau feuillé et boutons floraux (× 2/3); 2, fleur épanouie avec étamines à longs filets (× 6); 3, fleur à étamines courtes et style exsert (× 6); 4, jeune ovaire (× 10); 5, diagramme. — 1, Le Testu 1398; 2 à 5, Pobeguin 56.

synandrodaphne:paradoxa.caption: PL. craterosiphon:soyauxii.caption: PL. VIII. — Craterosiphon Soyauxii H.H.W. Pears. : 1, rameau florifère (× 2/3); 2, fragment de rameau (× 3); 3, fleur ouverte (× 3); 4, disque hypogyne (× 8); 5, fruit (× 2/3); 6, graine de face et de profil, (× 2/3). — 1 à 4, Le Testu 9626; 5 et 6, Soyaux 263, type.

dicranolepis:polygaloides.caption: PL. IX. — Dicranolepis polygaloides Gilg ex Pears. dicranolepis:pusilla.caption: PL. XII.—Dicranolepis pusilla G. Aym. (Le Testu 8142) : 1, rameau florifère (× 2 /3); 2, fragment de feuille (× 2); 3, fleur épanouie (× 3); 4, fruit mùr (× 1). —Dicranolepis Baertsiana, De Wild. et Dur. (Le Testu 5108); 5, rameau florifère (× 2 /3); 6, fruit (× 1).

genera_thymelaeaceae.name: KEY TO GABONESE OR PERIGABONESE GENERA

1.text: Androecium diplostemonous (flowers possessing a number of stamens double that of the divisions of the perianth).

2.text: Flowers with perianth (perigone) not tubular; external cycle (calyx) appearing dialisepalous (lobes free or united only slightly at the base).

3.text: Sepals all identical, arranged in a single circle; flowers in axillary glomerules; petals deeply bifid appearing as small squamiform lamellae smaller than the sepals..... I. Octolepis.

3'.text: Sepals of two kinds, 2 external, 2 internal; flowers in involucrate terminal heads; no petals, but an androecial tube on the outside of which the stamen filaments are united..... 2. Synandrodaphne.

2'.text: Flowers with perianth (perigone) tubular, divided into 4-5 lobes at its apex.

4.text: Tube of perianth not articulate-circumscissile above the ovary.

6.text: Petals absent (in general); tube fine at its base, widened into a funnel..... 3. Craterosiphon.

6'.text: Petals present as a fleshy circle at the throat of the tube...... (Synaptolepis, western and eastern Africa).

6''.text: Ten petaloid lamellary lobes inserted at the throat of the tube and longer or shorter than the sepals.... 4. Dicranolepis.

5'.text: Flowers quite long-pedicellate, grouped in an umbelliform inflorescence itself pedunculate..... 5. Peddiea.

5\"\".text: Flowers grouped in densely involucrate terminal capitules, with involucral bracts persisting after flowering..... ..... Gnidia glauca.

4'.text: Tube of perianth (perigone) articulate-circumscissile above the ovary (suture very visible even on immature flowers); flowers grouped in inflorescences surrounded by bracts..... 6. Gnidia.

8.text: Involucre normally with more than 5 bracts.....(ex-genus Gnidia s.s.

8'.text: Involucre with 2-3 bracts not very different from the upper leaves..... (ex-genus Englerodaphne, East Africa).

1'.text: Androecium isostemonous (stamens in number equal to that of the perianth divisions)..... Struthiola (East African Mountains).

1.text: Fruit dehiscent, opening in 2-4 valves, independent of the perianth.

2.text: Fruit with 2 septa, opening into two subequal valves.....

2'.text: Fruit with 1-4 septa, opening by irregular valves; generally a single fertile seed..... I. Octolepis.

3.text: Fruit dry, small (less than 5 mm), remaining at maturity in the base of the perigone..... 6. Gnidia.

4.text: Fruit bursting the perigone during its growth... 5. Peddiea.

4'.text: Pericarp of the fruit concrescent with the base of the perigone which becomes hypertrophied.

5'.text: Fruit spherical, with fibrous or fleshy mesocarp, with sclerified endocarp..... 4. Dicranolepis.

1.text: Flowers predominantly tetramerous; leaves 5-30 cm long, but generally more than 15, by 4-10 wide, with lamina rather attenuate-cuneate at the base, with 8-15 (and up to 20) pairs of lateral veins.....1. O. casearia. (leaves 8-12 cm long by 3-4 wide, fairly thick shagreened, with few veins..... O. nodosericea).
1.terminal_name: O. casearia. (leaves 8-12 cm long by 3-4 wide, fairly thick shagreened, with few veins..... O. nodosericea)

1'.text: Flowers predominantly pentamerous; leaves 5-20 cm long, but generally 10-12, by 2-7 cm wide, with base rather rounded or subcordate, with 5-12 pairs of lateral veins..... 2. O. decalepis.

1.text: Filaments of the epigepalous stamens very distinctly longer than the anthers and reaching 3 to 4 mm; stamens, style and stigma clearly salient from the perianth tube.

2.text: Divisions of the perianth nearly as long as the tube..... ..... C. Louisii R. Wilczek ex A. Robyns (Central Congo).
2.terminal_name: ..... C. Louisii R. Wilczek ex A. Robyns (Central Congo)

2'.text: Divisions of the perianth shorter than the tube.

3.text: Open flowers approximately 20-22 mm long; petals reaching 8 mm; bark with crevassed rhytidome; leaves coriaceous, small, 3-5 × 1.5-2 cm, attenuate at the base. C. Quarrei Staner (Katanga).

3'.text: Open flowers 15 to 18 mm long; petals approximately 5 mm; dark bark, adorned with numerous lenticels, not crevassed; coriaceous leaves, fairly large (5-8 × 3-5 cm), very clearly rounded at the base..... I. C. cf. Soyauxii.

1'.text: Filaments of the epigepalous stamens barely longer than the anthers, rarely exceeding 1 mm; stamens, style and stigma included or barely salient at the throat of the tube.

4.text: Zone of the tube throat not marked by thickenings corresponding to the petals. Leaves stiff or coriaceous, very ovate, only slightly longer than broad, fairly large 5 to 8 cm long by 4 to 5 wide; greatest width often situated near the base; blade gradually narrowed at the apex into a fairly broad triangular apex, 1 cm long.

5.text: Flowers grouped by 3 or 4, fairly large and reaching 4 cm..... C. scandens Engl. et Gilg (Western Africa).
5.terminal_name: C. scandens Engl. et Gilg (Western Africa)

5'.text: Flowers solitary, small, rarely reaching 2 cm.

6.text: Floral buds acute at the apex..... 2. C. montanum.

6'.text: Floral buds obtuse at the apex..... 3. C. pseudoscandens.

4'.text: Zone of the tube throat marked by 10 more or less pronounced thickenings corresponding to 10 rudimentary petaloid pieces; ovate oblong leaves, fairly small, 3.5 to 6 cm long, 2 to 2.5 wide, with greatest width situated rather towards the middle, gradually narrowed at the base, more or less abruptly narrowed at the apex into a triangular or subfiliform apex.... C. beniense Domke (Congo).

1.text: Perianth tube furnished with stipitate glands which persist on the fruit..... I. D. glandulosa.

3.text: Large flowers, often exceeding 35 mm in length, with petals longer than the sepals or equaling them, with narrow perianth tube.

4.text: Lobes of the petals distinctly lacerate-fimbriate.. 2. D. pulcherrima

4'.text: Lobes of the petals entire or somewhat dentate, with distal part enlarged into a spatula, considerably exceeding the sepals, the latter rounded at the apex..... D. grandiflora Engl.

4\"'.text: Lobes of the petals slightly dentate, acute; sepals lanceolate acute..... 3. D. Baertsiana.

3'.text: Medium flowers, 25–30 mm in length, with petals most often shorter than the sepals, with thick perianth tube, enlarged at the base..... D. vestita Engl.

3''.text: Flowers less than 25 mm, with very narrow tube..... 4. D. Soyauxii.

2'.text: Perianth tube pubescent-grayish, with fairly short and stiff hairs, not silky-hairy (see also 2'').

5.text: Flowers more than 25 mm, with petals equal to or exceeding the sepals in general.

6.text: Flowers shorter than the leaves, the latter very ovate, slightly oblique..... 3. D. Baertsiana.

6'.text: Flowers almost as long or longer than the leaves,

5'.text: Flowers less than 25 mm, with petals almost always shorter than the sepals, with a very narrow tube; leaves densely hairy-hirsute on lower surface, with stiff and long hairs.... 4. D. Soyauxii.

2''.text: Perigone tube with scattered appressed hairs, or entirely glabrous.

7.text: Flowers more than 20 mm, often reaching one-third or one-half the length of the leaves, with lobes of petals equaling the sepals..... 5. D. disticha.

8.text: Flowers reaching 1/4 or 1/3 of the length of the leaves, with lobes of petals smaller than the sepals... 6. D. Buchholzii.

8'.text: Flowers exceeding one-half the length of the leaves, with lobes of petals almost equaling the sepals; leaves very small, without individualized acumen..... 7. D. pusilla.

1.text: Plants with inflorescences situated almost entirely at the end of simple, erect, scarcely lignified branches, arising from a thick rootstock; chamaephytic plants or theophytes becoming woody.

2.text: Inflorescences small, about 10 mm in diameter, surrounded by few bracts (4-6), and short (10 mm).

3.text: Bracts narrow or acicular..... G. Claessensii Staner.

3'.text: Bracts very broadly ovate-obtuse..... G. macrorrhiza Gilg.

2'.text: Inflorescences 20-30 mm in diameter, bearing approximately 6-10 bracts (or more), these 10-25 mm.

4.text: Inflorescence complex, with flowers interspersed with numerous bracts..... G. bambutana Gilg et Lederm. ex Engl.
4.terminal_name: G. bambutana Gilg et Lederm. ex Engl

4'.text: Inflorescence comprising only an external involucre of bracts.

5.text: Bracts fairly little differentiated from the leaves in form, broad, obtuse at the apex..... G. Kraussiana (Meisn.) Gilg.

5'.text: Bracts differentiated from the leaves, acute-apiculate at the apex. …… G. chrysantha (Solms-Laub. ex Schweinf.) Gilg.

1'.text: Plants with terminal inflorescences situated at the end of woody, branched branches; phanerophyte (shrub or tree)..... G. glauca (Fres.) Gilg.

6.text: Main stems strongly lignified at the time of flowering; inflorescences borne by short lateral branchlets; bracts foliaceous, green..... G. foliosa Pears. [West Africa only]

6'.text: Main stems scarcely lignified at the time of flowering; bracts membranaceous, colored.

7.text: Bracts very broadly ovate; main stem bearing very regular lateral ramifications, oblique, terminated by drooping inflorescences..... G. Schweinfurthii Gilg.

7'.text: Bracts lanceolate-apiculate; inflorescences more or less sessile appearing axillary..... G. apiculata (Oliv.) Gilg.

8.text: Flowers grouped in terminal inflorescences; bracts lanceolate apiculate; flowers rather large exceeding the bracts; stems unbranched..... G. chrysantha.

8'.text: Flowers grouped in lateral inflorescences borne by short branchlets or short lateral peduncles; flowers small, scarcely exceeding the bracts, the latter ovate, foliaceous. G. foliosa.
